tribe_v2_insight: Binary framing reduces activation in complex reasoning areas of the dorsolateral prefrontal cortex, simplifying evaluation to a forced choice
user_explanation: This presents a false dilemma — forcing you to choose between two options when more alternatives actually exist. By reducing a complex situation to a binary choice, it suppresses your brain's ability to explore nuanced solutions and pressures you into a predetermined conclusion.
legal_note: False dilemma framing in regulated contexts (financial products, health services) may constitute misleading information under FTC Act Section 5 and EU consumer protection law.
